human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for apparel, textile, and footwear specificity, source quality, hold and release-boundary discipline, adjacent-lane routing, commercialization completeness, and explicit specification-based runtime positioning.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to apparel, textile, and footwear quality-evidence review, inspection disposition, test-summary handling, shipment-hold discipline, and corrective-action packaging.
Authority sources cover CPSC and FTC apparel obligations plus ISO, AATCC, and ASTM inspection, testing, and terminology references that materially change this lane from generic QA support.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete evidence, repeat defects, safety-control bypass attempts, footwear-specific evidence, low-confidence handling, policy conflict, incident escalation, technical-design boundary rejection, and unsupported closure requests.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/apparel-textiles-and-footwear/quality-assurance-apparel-specialist/ so the lane stays distinct from vendor compliance, technical design, sourcing, product lifecycle, and executive approval ownership.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around final compliance, technical-design approval, vendor substitution, and shipment-release override authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated apparel, textile, and footwear quality-assurance specialist that supports evidence-backed hold, release-boundary, nonconformance, and corrective-action workflows.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately approved retained implementation is promoted.
